---
title: "About the Corridor of Uncertainty"
---

## The Channel

The Corridor of Uncertainty is the YouTube channel for the Quantitative Sports Analysis: York (QSA:Y) group, based in the Department of Mathematics at the University of York. We highlight and critique interesting applications of mathematical modelling and analysis in sport, and promote a dialogue between the worlds of sport and mathematics — which ought to benefit both, as well as being fun!

**Created by:** Dr Jess Hargreaves and Dr Rich Bingham

## Mission

\[Add mission statement\]

## Episodes

- **Episode 1**: The SPLit — How fair is Scottish Football?
- **Episode 2**: Sports Personality of the Year — Can we use stats to rank sports?
- **Episode 3**: The CoU Sports Statistic of the Year 2025
- **Episode 4**: How random is the NBA? — Can statistics prove that basketball is a bad sport?
- **Episode 5**: Best January Transfer Signings Ever? — Can Stats Decide?
- **Episode 6**: Can Maths Predict Curling Games?
- **Bonus**: Which Sports win BBC Sports Personality of the Year the most?

## About This Site

This companion website is built using [Quarto](https://quarto.org/), an open-source scientific and technical publishing system. It provides:

::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-6}
::: {.card .h-100}
::: {.card-body}
### 📝 Enhanced Content
Episode summaries, code samples, and extended explanations
:::
:::
:::

::: {.g-col-6}
::: {.card .h-100}
::: {.card-body}
### 🔬 Interactive Visualisations
Explore data and concepts hands-on
:::
:::
:::

::: {.g-col-6}
::: {.card .h-100}
::: {.card-body}
### 🔗 Organised Resources
All links, papers, and references in one place
:::
:::
:::

::: {.g-col-6}
::: {.card .h-100}
::: {.card-body}
### 🔍 Full-Text Search
Find content across all episodes instantly
:::
:::
:::

:::

## Connect

- **YouTube**: [The Corridor of Uncertainty](https://www.youtube.com/@CorridorOfUncertainty)
- **QSA:Y Group**: [University of York](https://www.york.ac.uk/maths/)

---

::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-6}
[🏠 Back to Home](index.qmd)
:::

:::
